notes:
Mong đợi means to expect or look forward to something in the future. It often carries a hopeful feeling, not just a neutral prediction.

It’s a verb, usually followed by a noun or a clause about the future.  

It’s softer and more emotional than kỳ vọng (which sounds stronger and more formal).  
Opposite feeling: thất vọng (to be disappointed when what you expected doesn’t happen).

translation.en: I am looking forward to this trip.

example.en: I am looking forward to seeing you this weekend.
example.vi: Tôi mong đợi được gặp bạn vào cuối tuần.
example.es: Estoy deseando verte este fin de semana.

definitions.vi: Chờ đợi hoặc mong muốn điều gì đó với sự háo hức.
definitions.es: Anticipar o esperar algo con entusiasmo.
definitions.en: To anticipate or await something with eagerness.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I expect the exam results will be announced next week.
She always expect something good will come in life.
Everyone in the company expect the year-end party will be very fun.

translation.en: I am looking forward to this trip.

notes_md:
**Mong đợi** means *to expect* or *look forward to* something in the future. It often carries a hopeful feeling, not just a neutral prediction.

It’s a verb, usually followed by a noun or a clause about the future.  

It’s softer and more emotional than **kỳ vọng** (which sounds stronger and more formal).  
Opposite feeling: **thất vọng** (to be disappointed when what you expected doesn’t happen).
exampleSentences: Anh mong đợi kết quả của kỳ thi sẽ được công bố vào tuần sau.
Chị luôn mong đợi một điều gì đó tốt đẹp sẽ đến trong cuộc sống.
Mọi người trong công ty mong đợi buổi tiệc cuối năm sẽ rất vui.
